title: Started UE5 (v5.2)
bullets: Blueprint chaos, spaghetti graphs all over the place
First multiplayer prototype after 2 weeks (many bugs learned)
Didn't understand the difference between TArray and TSet (learned the hard way)
Thought 'Autorender' was a good subsystem name (it wasn't)

title: C++ Integration Breakthrough
bullets: "Expose to Blueprint" enlightenment moment - finally bridging C++ and BP
First proper UCLASS with correct garbage collection setup
Learned UFUNCTION() vs. native C++ methods
Started seeing Blueprints as glue, not the entire system

title: Systems Architecture Deep Dive
bullets: Realized component composition > inheritance (life-changing)
Built reusable ability system using GAS
Learned to profile with Unreal Insights (no more guesswork)
Started answering UE questions on forums (reputation building)

title: Optimization & Performance
bullets: Multithreading with AsyncTasks and task graphs
GPU profiling and shader complexity reduction
Package size optimization: 847 MB → 312 MB (63% reduction)
Implemented LODs, instancing, and async streaming

title: First Shipped Prototype
bullets: 3-month solo project from design to QA to patch
End-to-end pipeline: UE5 → Steam/Epic submission
Learned store requirements, ratings, and update process
First real player feedback (both good and bad)

title: Senior Gameplay Programmer (Target)
bullets: Deep diving into GAS advanced features, network prediction, and rendering
Contributing to Unreal Engine forums (50+ answers, 10k+ reputation)
Building YouTube tutorial series on UE5 gameplay systems
Mentoring junior developers on best practices
Exploring UE5's rendering pipeline for next-gen effects
